reviews.review_description:
The game is relatively simple but extremely fun. Feels like classic zombies and the difficulty is actually challenging! 

One thing I noticed is that power ups spawn very infrequently and the difficulty ramps up rather quickly. As a solo player it proved to be pretty hard to get past a certain round. Haven’t tried multiplayer but I can imagine this is even better with friends (either in the same space or online). 

Also, keep in mind when you’re scanning your room and it asks you to create a boundary for every item/furniture that’s in your way, please mark EVERYTHING. Not only does it add to the aesthetic of your defensive area by creating in-game representations of your stuff, but I forgot to scan my cat’s litter robot and almost put my hand in it trying to repair a fence 😅

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the fantastic review! 😊

We're glad you enjoyed both the challenge and the room-adaptive gameplay.

Your feedback about power-up frequency and solo balancing is very valuable and something we'll continue to monitor as we add new updates.

And thanks for mentioning the room scan! We definitely recommend scanning everything in the room. Besides making the environment feel more immersive, it also helps avoid accidental encounters with real-world objects... including litter robots 😅

Hope you'll get a chance to try the multiplayer mode as well!

reviews.review_description: This game is a blast! The graphics are great and the game play is fun. Just be sure to do the tutorial before playing and make sure to setup your zone correctly.  Zombie waves are great to play with friends and family.  Working together to keep your fences up is a great way to do basic team building.  Give this a shot, you won’t be disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Love that title 😄
Thanks for the great feedback! We’re happy you enjoyed playing together with friends and family — teamwork is a big part of what we wanted to achieve. And great tip about the tutorial and zone setup!

reviews.review_description:
Good entertaining game that lets you play around a large play area like your house (be sure to map it correctly so you don't run into anything).  
Challenging when playing as single player because if you make a large area you need to be quick to repair the fences area.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review!
Using larger play areas definitely adds to the immersion — and the challenge 😅 Glad you enjoyed the experience, and thanks for the helpful notes for other players!

reviews.review_title: Great defend game without the dizziness!
reviews.review_description: This is a great defend shooter game that won't induce any motion sickness because you're moving in your own environment! Tons of fun solo, and feels like it would be even more fun in multi-player, just need to have more headset so can play together!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the kind words! 🙌
Motion sickness–free gameplay was one of our main goals, so it’s great to hear that it works for you. And yes — multiplayer really shines when more headsets are around 😉 Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description:
So I'll ask directly, explain to me why i need a whole additional PC and to pay $0.46 a player to run your crap server to play more than 2 players on this game? When half the marketplace is now indie developers giving away gorilla tag clones with comparable graphics and more effort put into gameplay than this game, why do you think it's okay to paywall extra players into(with a ridiculous amount of setup work) into your colocation methodology? 

Paywalling then advertising their COMMERCIAL USE server subscription in a consumer game to play more than 2 players is insane, and in blatant violation of the dev terms of use for Meta, unless there is some strange loophole in the terms that allowed this BS because of how they set up the quest 2's mapping structure.

developer_response.body:
Edit: After a lot of hard work, we’ve re-implemented the option for 4-player support and adjusted the description accordingly (see the latest update). We’d really appreciate it if you could update your review.
Wishing you a wonderful Christmas time!

Hi there!
We're really sorry to hear about your disappointing experience with HouseDefender, and we appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts.

To clarify a few things:
- HouseDefender is designed and advertised on the Meta Store as a 2-player co-op game - both in terms of gameplay and its built-in co-location mechanics. We don’t state anywhere that the game supports 2–4 players in the Store. If you’ve seen that elsewhere, we’d really appreciate it if you could let us know where so we can address it.

- For setups with more than 2 players, we developed SyncArena, a separate solution for the Location-Based Entertainment (LBE) market. These environments require completely different technical setups, including external hardware, networking and spectator view. Offering SyncArena is meant to provide professional users with options, not to “paywall” home players. We understand it can be confusing, and we're working on making the distinction even clearer.

We truly value your feedback - it helps us improve. If there's anything unclear or you'd like to talk more, feel free to reach out directly in our discord: https://discord.gg/enstvvYc

Thanks again for playing HouseDefender, and we hope you’ll give it another shot in the future!

reviews.review_description: This is a great time!

developer_response.body: We’re really glad you’re having a great time with the game!

reviews.review_title: Great Mixed Reality / VR Wave Shooter
reviews.review_description:
This game is a lot of fun. I played single player and I imagine multiplayer makes it even better. They just recently added 4 player co-op. 

The graphics and gameplay are great and setting up your play area is easy. Watching your house get turned into a VR landscape was really cool. So far the game has two environments each with their own powerups and varied enemies. You have to repair your fence if damaged between rounds. See how long you can survive before you are overwhelmed. 

The devs continue to provide support and updates for this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the great review! 😊

We're happy to hear you enjoyed the room transformation and the mix of defense, repair, and survival gameplay.

Supporting and improving House Defender over time is very important to us, so it's especially nice to hear that the updates are being noticed.

We hope you'll get a chance to try the new 4-player co-op mode soon! 🧟‍♂️🏹

reviews.review_title: Creative, Fun, and Motion Sickness Free!
reviews.review_description:
Today, I played House Defender for the first time, and as a VR newbie, I was blown away by its unique mechanics. The ability to place fences around your play space to defend against skeletons, bomb monsters, and more is super creative and immersive. To my surprise, the game didn’t make me motion sick at all, which is a huge plus, considering how many VR games can be rough on new players.

The gameplay itself is fast-paced and challenging, with waves of monsters that get progressively harder. Completing waves gives you the chance to unlock new weapons, keeping the action fresh and exciting. Overall, House Defender has quickly become one of my new favorite VR games. I highly recommend giving it a try!

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you for such a detailed and thoughtful review! ❤️
We’re especially happy to hear that the game felt comfortable even as a VR newbie. Keeping movement natural and immersive is exactly what we’re aiming for. Thanks for playing and recommending House Defender!

reviews.review_title: Impressive MR, basic wave defender demo
reviews.review_description:
I was looking for precisely what this game offers - the ability to walk around my apartment and experience another world. This style of MR is still hard to find, but this dev has implemented it super well. It works just like it says on the tin. The coolest thing for me is seeing across your whole home without walls getting in the way. This dev’s other work follows the same premise, although this is the only one with the excellent manual mapping wizard. Maybe future updates to the others will let you import your map?
My only wish is that these games had a bit more depth to them. A base building wave/prep gameplay loop a la Bloons or PvZ would be nice. Or maybe a richer upgrade scheme. Or a few puzzle elements. Lots of things could work - I just want more details to make me want to stay in the world. Wave survival like this is a game mode that always ends in failure, and i’d like to be able to enjoy the room scale setup in a slightly more satisfying way

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for your valuable feedback. Nice ideas! :)
We're working on an update and we'd be delighted if you'd check back again.

reviews.review_description: Great value. I received this as a gift and immediately purchased the bundle. It was a riot. It's all great fun until you can't get your fences/boundaries repaired. As someone who spooks easily in VR, this was absolutely not scary-until you turn around and there's a creature behind you. Multiplayer mode is the most fun I've had playing with family in VR. I can't wait to see where this goes.  You won't be sorry you purchased. Lots of the earlier glitches I read about are gone. Didn't require any additional money for multiplayer in December 2025 as stated in an earlier review. Did I say it was FUN?

update: Hi developers, in this latest update you have included Meta's room tracking but you have completely eliminated your tracking system which in my opinion was still great! and in my opinion it is worth keeping both of them in the game, letting the player choose which of the two systems to use! do you know why? because with your tracking in addition to being immediate and quite simple, it has the advantage of being able to manage things a bit as you want, so for example add obstacles even where there aren't any to create a more particular map, something you can't do with Meta's room tracking. so I recommend keeping both of them.




Update:


I like the new update. the game did a good job with the virtualization of real furniture.

do you have a Discord server where you can provide feedback, debugging, and ideas?

I would like to be able to choose different hands and not always have skeleton hands. furthermore, once they are inside I would like to be able to punch them again, and defend myself as much as possible, I would also like to be able to grab them by the arm and throw them away (the lighter ones obviously)

in addition to reloading the gun with a button I would like to have a reload in which I make a movement with my arm. 

while regarding the fence, have you also tried to make the player do a sort of movement with the simulated hammer? instead of moving it left and right up and down also that the player should give virtual hammers?

It's good that Meta's room tracking is not used, so the user can decide independently to create different shapes. 

After making the boundaries of the room you can choose to make furniture boundaries larger or that add other shapes that are not there while maintaining the furniture boundaries of the room

I tried it yesterday and the fact that you can create different shapes when setting up the room improves the experience while playing

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the valuable feedback. Please share your feedback always on our discord server to directly communicate with our developers :)
https://discord.gg/enstvvYc

reviews.review_description: Tell me...why is my game not updating? Why can't I go online and make a room? It's sad (the game is awesome...I can't play.

developer_response.body: Hey, sorry to hear! Unfortunately I am not sure what happens here. Does the probleme still exists? Can you provide a better explanation please? We'd be delighted if you'd check back again.

reviews.review_description: Fun but the fence fixing takes far too long in comparison to how quickly enemies destroy it. Also would be better with weapon upgrades as you progress through levels. Also the room scaling could be more practical

reviews.review_title: Shooting is fun, fixing fences stinks.
reviews.review_description: This is great exercise and I really love this game except for one thing . . . I can't repair the fences fast enough. I know you can play with other people and maybe if there was more than just me one person could shoot and one person could fix the fences, but with just me, I can't get past the third level.

Setting up environment is troublesome if you have lots of furniture in your home, and elevated surfaces. I couldn't get to start playing because i had things blocking fences for repairing. Game says that i could not reach, but i could walk freely to the fence and repair it if needed

developer_response.body: We are really sorry that you run into problems. The problem appears if the system can't find a minimum 1-meter free wall space where it can place an attackable fence. To solve the issue please try it again with another room or exclude some objects by adding virtual walls around them. More info you find here at the FAQ:  https://2sync.io/housedefender/ If the problem still occurs you can also refund the game.

reviews.review_description: It’s a really nice demo. Was a little hard to set up the environnement but we made it. For a payed app I would have expected a little more…
I wish we could have 2 guns, more time or a faster way to fix those fences. It’s fun, but limited.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for your feedback. We've released an update that adds a few things. We'd be delighted if you'd check back again :)

reviews.review_description: One of the few games that includes local co-op, which is a lot of fun even if the tracking isn't perfect. It would be very nice if there were more level worlds and different weapons.

developer_response.body: thanks for the feedback! We had a small break, but we start working on new levels and weapons soon :)

reviews.review_description: Impressive

reviews.review_title: Whole new level of VR experiences. Cool!
reviews.review_description: Wow such a cool concept. Scanning is surprisingly fast and then I can run and play with friends all over the apartment. It's really a lot of fun! I'm excited to see what new features are coming. More powerups would be cool
